---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    grid:
      margin-width: 180px
      body-width: 780px
    theme: cosmo
---

<style>
.project-block {
  padding: 1.5rem 0;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e9ecef;
}

.project-title {
  margin-top: 0;
}

.section-header {
  margin-top: 3rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

img {
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 6px 18px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);
}
</style>


## Drunk Driving Surveillance Dashboard

::: {.project-block}
::: {.grid .align-items-center}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

**Overview:**  
The Drunk Driving Surveillance Dashboard is a publicly available tool designed to monitor alcohol-related crashes in El Paso. It includes over 30 indicators covering crash trends, geographic patterns, and driver and vehicle characteristics. The dashboard is updated monthly, enabling near real-time monitoring of impaired driving in the city.

<br>

<a href="crash_dashboard.html" target="_blank" class="btn btn-primary">
View Dashboard
</a>

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

![](screenshot of dashboard.jpg)

:::
:::
:::

---

## In the Media

::: {.project-block}
::: {.grid .align-items-center}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

**Opinion Article:**  
This piece explores how crime prevention strategies are communicated to the public and how media narratives shape perceptions of safety and deterrence in El Paso. It reflects on the role of transparency, messaging, and public engagement in addressing drunk driving and related harms.

<br>

<a href="https://elpasomatters.org/2026/02/25/opinion-why-el-paso-needs-better-data-to-solve-drunk-driving-problem/" target="_blank" class="btn btn-primary">
Read Article
</a>

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

![](opinion_article.jpg)

:::
:::
:::

---

## Working Papers & Reports

The El Paso Crime Lab produces applied research and technical reports that examine crime trends, public safety issues, and policy impacts in the region. These reports translate data into clear, evidence-based insights for policymakers, practitioners, and the public.

---

### Does Public Shaming Deter Drunk Driving?

::: {.project-block}
::: {.grid .align-items-center}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

**Summary:**  
This study evaluates the impact of a social media campaign in El Paso that publicly shared booking photos of individuals arrested for driving while intoxicated (DWI). Using Bayesian structural time-series methods and multiple comparison groups, the analysis isolates the campaign’s effect on alcohol-related crashes.

Results suggest the campaign was associated with reductions in alcohol-related crashes, particularly among drivers aged 31 and older. The findings highlight both the potential and limits of public-facing deterrence strategies.

<br>

<a href="reports/dwi_study.pdf" download class="btn btn-primary">
Download Paper (PDF)
</a>

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

![](dwi_study.jpg)

:::
:::
:::
